title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
author: Doyle, Arthur Conan
summary: Sherlock Holmes and Dr. John Watson investigate a dozen compact mysteries in Victorian London. Holmes’s extraordinary powers of observation, deduction, disguise, and forensic knowledge unravel puzzles involving thefts, impersonations, secret leagues, domestic tragedies, jealousies, and bizarre devices. Cases include the cunning Irene Adler and the politically delicate Scandal in Bohemia; the Red‑Headed League’s absurd plot; the lethal Speckled Band; the Blue Carbuncle recovered from a Christmas goose; the engineer’s severed thumb; and other intrigues that reveal human weakness, cunning, and courage. Watson narrates with admiration and warmth, recording Holmes’s methods, triumphs, and occasional bemused detachment. His fame endures globally still

characters.name: Sherlock Holmes
characters.personality: brilliant, observant, analytical, aloof, occasionally playful
characters.lifespan: Active throughout the cases; survives and continues detective work

characters.name: Dr. John Watson
characters.personality: loyal, steady, empathetic, chronicler of Holmes’s cases
characters.lifespan: Narrator of the adventures; survives and records Holmes’s exploits

characters.name: Irene Adler
characters.lifespan: Outwits Holmes in 'A Scandal in Bohemia'; marries and leaves public life

characters.name: Count Von Kramm (alias)
characters.lifespan: Appears as masked client in 'A Scandal in Bohemia'; seeks Holmes’s help

characters.name: Trepoff
characters.personality: victim in a foreign case referenced by Holmes
characters.lifespan: Referenced as a past case Holmes was summoned to (Odessa)

characters.name: Atkinson brothers
characters.personality: victims in a singular tragedy referenced by Holmes
characters.lifespan: Referenced as a past case at Trincomalee

characters.name: House of Ormstein (reigning family)
characters.lifespan: Mentioned as implicated in a delicate matter in the visit

characters.name: Boswell (reference)
characters.personality: mentioned metaphorically as Watson’s chronicling role
characters.lifespan: Not a character in the narrative; used as an analogy
